name: Nightmare Excursion
Description:
I have pinpointed an area of the Labyrinth where a strange confluence of demonic energy is occurring, but it is too deep within its winding pathways for me to venture to safely. With you being human, however, you should be at much less of a risk.

I suspect the confluence is caused by an object of some sort, though I shan't conjecture as to its exact nature. Should you be able to get ahold of this item, it shall both help with my research and, if I am not mistaken, allow your Grimoire to mimic the empowering effects of the Labyrinth onto any location you visit, should you wish to challenge yourself.

I hope this excites your attention, and hope for your collaboration in this matter.♥
- Ushris, Caretaker of the Labyrinth, Barghest Witch of the Umbral Coven

theScene: After venturing past the myriad of encounters, and walking down the Labyrinth's twisting darkened halls further and further into suffocating darkness, you're now quite sure you have reached your destination, your current area oozing an almost tangible feeling of heart-pounding pressure, which must have to do with the 'confluence' that Ushris' briefing mentioned.

Taking a deep breath to steady yourself and gather your adventurous courage, you proceed onward, tension filling your chest as you make your way towards a faintly glowing purple light, the black walls around you feeling more and more twisted the closer you get to the source of the eldritch radiance, as if frozen in a half-melted inky state.

Entering the chamber ahead, you find a small cylindrical room, surprisingly quiet and empty save for a tiny black prism floating in the center, glowing ever so slightly with palpable demonic energy, its surface seeming to swirl with starry specks, as if looking straight into a swatch of moonless sky.

There's a heavy, oppressive spiritual weight over your body as you trudge closer, the feeling of anxiety within you tightening into a twisted knot as you keep nervously looking around, expecting that at any moment, some unknown defender of this place will lunge out of hiding or materialize out of thin air to milk you dry.

Yet, even once you have gotten within arm's reach of the jewel-like object and the sensation of pressure has become so strong that it almost feels like you're getting pulled in... there is only dead eerie silence, the strange shard not even making a sound as it continues to hover still in the air.
Figuring that you're getting nowhere just standing in place, you do your best to push back the persistent intuition that this is a bad idea, and reach out to grab your prize...

The moment you touch its surface, however, you are suddenly assailed with a strange sense of vertigo, like the floor just gave out from under you, leaving you tumbling into a vast void of nothingness, falling into a gaping endless maw from which there is no escape, your stomach abruptly lurching in confused panic as sensations and images rush through you.

Flashes of {i}something{/i} trickle into your mind: fires and smoke, echoing screams, the skies gaping before you, a hollow pit of despair in your chest, yelling, angry voices, ice-cold eyes boring into you, rushing crashing arcs of red lightning, pain, shock, the rush of falling, anguish, enclosing imprisoning darkness, deafening silence... Forever and ever and ever and ever and ever and ever and ever and ever and ever andeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverandeverISEEYOU.

You exhale and blink, breathless. Eyes. So many eyes in the endless void watching you as you fall. Base panic surges in your body and mind, your every instinct fruitlessly shouting at you that {i}you have to get out of here{/i}. Repressing the urge to shriek, you look around like a cornered animal, but find nowhere to run. There are countless hands reaching out towards you from below. A trill of laughter brushes across your ears.

Something is coming for you. You know it would take only a downward glance to know what, and despite the abject fear clutching at every inch of your body, your morbid curiosity and frayed nerves get the better of you and you look down-

With a start and a gasp, you shudder in place, suddenly finding yourself back in the halls of the Labyrinth, your heart still racing and sweat trailing down your skin as you laboriously regain your breathing in shaky, heaving gulps... Yet, despite your persistent agitation, you now feel... safe. Awake... Was that some kind of nightmarish vision caused by this place-? Opening your clenched hand, you see you're still holding that prism...

As if having become dormant once wrenched from its place of rest, or perhaps having exhausted whatever magic it was imbued with, the jewel-like object now looks like little more than an innocuous, speckled chunk of obsidian... But even then, you can't shake off the feeling that you're looking through a window into another world whenever you let your gaze rest on it for too long, the sensation both disquieting and darkly fascinating.

Shaking your head, you pocket the thing and, feeling no inclination to linger in these oppressive depths a moment longer, you immediately depart, all too eager to make your way back out of this place...

Thankfully, the Labyrinth holds no other spooky surprises for you this time, and you find your way back to its higher levels in mere moments thanks to your Grimoire and to Lucidia's ever-present dreamlike mode of accelerated travel.

Stepping out of an ascending stairway, you promptly find yourself in the familiar, elegantly no-nonsense confines of Ushris' floor, the witchy hellhound herself daintily sipping tea at her table just as usual, the quiet sight reassuringly confirming that you're back to normalcy, a soothing feeling of relief streaming through you as you finally let go of the tension you were still instinctively holding inside your body.
There you are, [ThePlayerName]. I take it things went well...?
She pauses and her expression shifts into one of alert concern as she takes in your haggard aspect, her ears flicking sympathetically as she nods in understanding.
Ah... It seems your venture was rougher than I anticipated... My sincerest apologies for that.

Breathing out a sigh, she lets her downcast expression shift into a comfortingly cheerful one despite a lingering air of self-reproach, the petite woman reaching out with a huge claw to invitingly tap the other side of the table in front of her.
... Why don't you take a seat, have a soothing cup of tea, and tell me about it all? You look like you've seen a ghost, and not the pleasant kind... What happened?

Definitely not feeling like turning down some rest and relaxation, you do so and, over a nice helping of the warm beverage, you impart to her what occurred and show her the strange prism, quite glad to be able to take a moment to let your agitation quiet down for good after the ordeal you just went through, looking silently at Ushris while she inspects your finding, the shard looking even smaller between two of her enormous fingers.

Hmm... I must say that touching it is not imparting me with anything like the visions you experienced... In fact, I am not experiencing anything at all, and cannot discern anything in particular save that this is a unique form of crystallization for demonic energy, of a kind that we are actually both quite familiar with: {i}'Lucidian'{/i} demonic energy... I would go as far as to say it is almost like this crystal is mimicking a sigil...
Like the ones from Sofia or the Oni Twins?

Just so! Those ones, however, are monster-made, and not with demonic energy, whereas this one very much appears to have {i}spontaneously{/i} come into existence... Unless it was as a result of some of the Labyrinth's long-lost functions...?

As for why or how it appeared, I must shamefully confess that your guess is as good as mine. This place is so ancient, advanced, and complex that its exact workings are a mystery, even to me... Though I would not put it past the Labyrinth for this crystallization to have been the result of some willful intent on the part of what very much feels like a sentient entity with a will of its own...

In fact, this is not the first 'nightmare prism' I've found within its confines, though it seems to be one of the most refined and solidly formed so far. As you experienced, it seems to have been originally instilled with some form of turbulent nightmare magic, though I am positive that this potency is now depleted, leaving no risk of it giving you any more strange waking nightmares.

The hellhound looks like she is thinking of something as she turns the shard this way and that between her surprisingly dexterous monster fingers, but then shakes her head and sets the crystal down on the table with a dull clink before lightly pushing it towards you with one of her claws, the item in question gently clattering across the table before rolling to a halt next to your now empty teacup.

Now, do take it back before I am tempted to buy it off you as a treat for me... It is essentially a dense chunk of raw demonic energy, after all, and would be quite useful and... {i}tasty{/i} for a witch like me...

But as for you, by virtue of its residual energy, its connection to this place, and Lucidia's own properties, putting it into your Grimoire will allow you to mimic the Labyrinth's empowering effects onto any area you henceforth find yourself in, imbuing your foes with sexual prowess to match yours... should you wish it.
Is it really safe, though? What about the... visions?
Hm? I take it they made a strong impression on you?

Worry not! Hihihi~ That was just the Labyrinth's magic preying on your subconscious primal fears... Much like any nightmare, it is very irrational and nonsensical, having little meaning save for the instinctual anxieties that any living, thinking being such as you or I feel.

Though- Do not get me wrong. I do not mean to diminish how harrowing this experience must have been for you. Having been through similar manifestations myself while traveling the Labyrinth's depths, I cannot but agree that they are wholly unpleasant, and do not blame you for feeling rattled. Walking in darkened tunnels only to find oneself plummeting into some abyss below... Dreams of falling really are the worst...

While her snakelike tail opens its mouth to moodily groan as if in confirmation, Ushris raises a claw in a froufrou of soft clothing punctuated by the clinking of her chains, gently reaching out across the table to delicately pat your hand and offer you a comforting smile.

And now, with your mission complete and my renewed thanks and apologies for what you've been through, perhaps you should head back to Town and take a breather? Do feel free to have a break, enjoy yourself, and get a good night's sleep. Relaxation and comfort do wonders for such things.
... Or maybe, instead, you can jump straight back into another adventure if you feel so inclined. Far be it from me to presume as to what works best for you, [ThePlayerName]~

You can't help but respond to her giggling banter with a smile, just like you can't help but agree with her suggestion: getting out of the Labyrinth's dark claustrophobic interior would do wonders for your mental state right now...

Thus, you part from the hellhound witch with your gem-like reward securely stashed in your inventory, your hostess seeing you off with a companionable wave as you step out of her room, then out of the Labyrinth itself, making your way back to Town without further incidents.
